//: Core Web Vitals budget for www.insolvia.ai (issue #46). Being dramatically
lighter and faster than a Flutter-web build is the entire reason this site
is React and not Flutter (CLAUDE.md 'Dual-target parity discipline' /
MVP_PLAN.md D4) — Flutter's CanvasKit runtime alone is ~1.5–2 MB of wasm
before a byte of app code. This budget is what keeps that reasoning true:
if the site ever fails it, the honest fix is to get lighter, not to raise
the numbers.
Run in CI by .github/workflows/marketing-pr.yml (npx @lhci/cli autorun,
cwd apps/insolvia_marketing, after npm run build). Runs locally the same
way. Lighthouse's default preset is *mobile* (emulated Moto G Power,
slow-4G network throttling, 4x CPU slowdown) — kept deliberately: it is
the harsher, more honest test, and the marketing site must be fast on a
phone, not just on an attorney's desktop.
The server under audit is scripts/lhci-serve.mjs — the real production
SSR server behind a proxy that stamps X-Forwarded-Host, so the SEO
audits (robots.txt included) see the production variant instead of the
deliberate non-production noindex (issue #48).
Budget rationale (measured 2026-07-21 on / and /waitlist: performance
0.98, accessibility 0.96, best-practices 0.96, SEO 1.00; LCP ~1.96 s;
script transfer ~125 KB; total ~136 KB):
- category minimums: performance 0.90, a11y 0.95, best-practices 0.90,
SEO 0.95 — floors, all currently passed.
- script transfer < 300 KB: the anti-Flutter line, with today's site at
~125 KB. CanvasKit could never fit under this.
- total transfer < 1 MB: whole-page weight cap, same reasoning.
- LCP < 2.5 s: Google's 'good' threshold, on the throttled mobile run;
today ~1.96 s.
- 3 runs, median asserted, to keep flaky throttling from failing PRs.

upload.//: Filesystem, not temporary-public-storage: no third-party uploads.
